_about: Staged amendment manifest for RFC-0034 + ADR-032 (DN-29 §11). Anchor-keyed, single-pass-per-file (see tools/dn29_apply.py). STAGED, NOT RUN: apply only AFTER both RFC-0034 and ADR-032 are Accepted (RFC-0034 §13). Anchors are unique content substrings, never line/offset, so order is irrelevant. Line numbers in DN-29 §11 drift — re-verify every anchor against source before completing this manifest.
_completion_note: Only the validated Foundation spine anchors (SC-3, FR-M3) are populated here as dry-run-clean worked examples. The remaining ~38 hits across ~12 files (DN-29 §11.2: README ~10, Foundation ~9, CLAUDE.md ~8, plus RFC-0001/0002/0005, Glossary, CONTRIBUTING, ADR footnotes) are authored at ratification, once RFC-0034 fixes the final wording. The tool refuses --apply while status != 'final'.

docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.note: SC-3 — unconditional 'zero swaps without a certificate' becomes per-mode; the mode stays never-silent (ADR-032 decision 2).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.anchor: - **SC-3 (no silent swaps).** Static + runtime invariant: zero representation swaps occur without producing an inspectable certificate (auditable; cross-cutting **A.1**, **G9**).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.replacement: - **SC-3 (no silent swaps).** Static + runtime invariant: at the active certification mode (`certified`), zero representation swaps occur without producing an inspectable certificate; in **every** mode the mode itself is never silent — each result is mode-tagged and `EXPLAIN`-able (auditable; cross-cutting **A.1**, **G9**; relaxed to per-mode by RFC-0034 + ADR-032).

docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.note: FR-M3 — 'always emits a certificate' becomes 'at the active mode'; swap stays never-silent (ADR-032 decision 2).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.anchor: - **FR-M3.** Provide an explicit `swap` operation that never runs implicitly and always emits a certificate (cross-cutting **A.1**; **G2**, **G9**).
docs/Mycelium_Project_Foundation.md.replacement: - **FR-M3.** Provide an explicit `swap` operation that never runs implicitly and, at the active mode (`certified`), emits a certificate; the swap is never-silent in every mode (cross-cutting **A.1**; **G2**, **G9**; relaxed to per-mode by RFC-0034 + ADR-032).
